sources_exist.note: Eleven of thirteen canonical occurrences were accessible. The PE Professional exit page exposed only an anti-bot holding page, and NAPPS returned an internal error, leaving the exit and pricing citations inaccessible.

figures_match_sources.note: The sources do not support 40% material AI adoption, the claimed LegalZoom/AI adoption analog, 45% of owners age 60+, 3x/8x EV/EBITDA, or the stated fee ranges. The cited SBA FAQ instead reports 16.3% of self-employed people age 65+ and 4.6% of all businesses using AI during March 2023-February 2024.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The declared fractions and pi values are bounded with task-level rationales, but the record never evidences that legal document preparation is the dominant revenue subsegment of this heterogeneous code. That unsupported composition assumption drives the 51.75% automatable share, 40% adoption, t50=3, and MELTING classification. LOW_CONFIDENCE remains unresolved.

rubric_consistent.note: All deterministic dataset inputs match exactly, and the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL differences on A, B, M, and S are benign stored-precision deltas. However, the rubric requires scoring the evidenced dominant subsegment; the record assigns the whole-code labor share and n_band to hypothesized document-prep firms without revenue-share evidence or a boundary check on LegalZoom's classification.

cross_checks_honest.note: The no-proxy answer cites a Qualia/ALTA adoption survey that is not actually cited anywhere in the record, and the pricing-deflation answer claims 100% per-transaction revenue even though the cited LegalZoom results report $492.5M subscription revenue versus $263.6M transaction revenue in 2025. The terminal answer likewise presents an unsupported subsegment as dominant.

source_audits.note: The release confirms Gridiron's August 2025 acquisition of ABC Legal, ABC's prior ownership by Aquiline, and organic/acquisition growth, but it does not document the named June 2025 add-ons, LegalZoom's Formation Nation transaction, an exhaustive market search, or count=2.

The page reports small-business sale multiples based on revenue and seller discretionary earnings, not lower-middle-market $1M-$5M EBITDA multiples of 3.6x-4.0x or normalized EV/EBITDA for process-serving/document-prep firms.

The release supports 2025 revenue of $756.0M, up 11%, and adjusted EBITDA of $172.2M. It does not describe an AI-first strategy, the claimed AI product rollout, 31% of legal professionals using generative AI, 21% of law firms adopting it, or 40% material production use in this subsegment.

Only an anti-bot 'One moment, please' holding page was accessible. No transaction terms or EBITDA multiple could be verified, and the record itself says ABC Legal's terms were undisclosed, so 8.0x is unsupported.

The article supports California CCP 413.30's January 1, 2026 electronic-service pathway and the Los Angeles Superior Court eService dates. It does not support LegalZoom reaching 50% consumer adoption by 2010, generative AI moving from 0% to 40% in legal document preparation during 2022-2025, or a t50 adoption pace.

The SBA FAQ reports that 16.3% of self-employed people were age 65+ in 2023. It does not contain the quoted 52.3% age 55+, 29.5% age 55-64, or 22.8% age 65+ owner figures and does not support 45% of 541199 owners being age 60+.

The release describes ABC Legal as a tech-enabled dual-sided platform connecting more than 29,000 clients with more than 8,000 process servers, directly supporting the platform-intermediation evidence used in the capture rationale.

The August 2025 NCSC paper expressly recommends modernizing UPL rules through vetted AI tools, regulatory sandboxes, or narrower definitions, supporting the asserted AI-driven regulatory pressure on nonlawyer legal-document services.

The release supports LegalZoom's scale but contradicts the claim that dominant segments have no recurring revenue: 2025 subscription revenue was $492.5M, versus $263.6M of transaction revenue. It does not support pi_dist=0.20.

This is guidance on automating repetitive court-administration tasks such as data entry, invoices, reports, and court filings. It does not establish low entry barriers for 541199 firms, process-server licensing, e-service substitution, patent-agent population, or pi_moat=0.20.

The article supports the limited California court-directed electronic-service pathway, Los Angeles court eService rollout, and AI-assisted intake/routing while distinguishing initial summons from later court-document service.

The NCSC paper confirms that current UPL rules can impede AI legal tools and advocates regulatory changes that could permit more AI-driven consumer legal assistance, supporting the bypass-threat portion of the moat rationale.

The cited NAPPS homepage returned an internal error and no fee survey or member resource was accessible. It therefore does not verify process-serving, document-preparation, or patent-agent fee ranges, billing mix, or the claim of entirely transaction-based revenue.

reasons: Re-run the heterogeneous-code composition analysis with sourced revenue shares and NAICS boundaries for legal document preparation, process serving, and patent-agent/specialty services before applying a dominant-subsegment role mix, adoption rate, and terminal class to the whole code.

Re-run inputs.current_adoption_pct and inputs.t50_years with an industry-specific survey of material production use; https://investors.legalzoom.com/news-releases/news-release-details/legalzoom-reports-strong-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025/ supports financial results but none of the quoted adoption percentages or the 40% estimate.

Replace inputs.historical_analogs with a citation supporting the actual LegalZoom/AI adoption series; https://countrywideprocess.com/blog/the-state-of-process-serving-in-2026-five-statutory-and-operational-changes-reshaping-the-legal-support-industry/ supports e-service dates, not 9-year LegalZoom adoption or 0%-to-40% generative-AI adoption.

Re-run inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult with disclosed EV/EBITDA transactions for comparable firms; BizBuySell reports SDE/revenue multiples, and the cited PE Professional page was inaccessible while ABC Legal's transaction terms were undisclosed.

Replace inputs.owners_60plus_pct with the correct cited age data or an industry-specific owner source; https://advocacy.sba.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Frequently-Asked-Questions-About-Small-Business_2024-508.pdf does not contain the quoted 52.3%, 29.5%, or 22.8% figures.

Re-run inputs.active_consolidators with dated evidence for each qualifying PE-backed add-on platform and a defensible code boundary; the ABC Legal release alone does not support both counted consolidators.

Source inputs.pricing_structure by segment and reconcile cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation with the cited LegalZoom results, which show majority subscription revenue rather than the asserted 100% per-transaction flat-fee mix.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S
